question: Mark–Matthew
conclusion: Matthew most likely used Mark or a Mark-like written text as a major narrative source.

question: Matthew–Luke double tradition
conclusion: The isolated non-Markan material is better explained by common written/oral Jesus tradition than by a simple one-way Matthew↔Luke dependence.

question: Q
conclusion: The data supports a shared sayings/tradition layer; it does not by itself prove one discrete Q document.

question: John–Synoptics
conclusion: John shares substantial tradition with the Synoptics, especially passion/sign material, but the data does not justify a simple "John copied X Gospel" model.

question: Thomas–Synoptics
conclusion: Thomas is best treated as a sayings-network witness, not as cleanly dependent on one canonical Gospel.

question: 1 Timothy 5:18 / Luke 10:7
conclusion: This is one of the strongest cases for a Luke-like written Jesus saying known to the author of 1 Timothy, though not necessarily canonical Luke in final form.

question: James / Gospel sayings
conclusion: James likely draws on Jesus tradition; direct use of Matthew or Luke is not demonstrated by the data.

question: Other apocrypha beyond Thomas
conclusion: The package does not yet support firm conclusions; it mostly identifies future high-value texts.
